chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# Chapter 17: Getting Started: The First Week
# ==============================================================================
# Module-level intent for the seventeenth chapter of the IVD book. The practical
# adoption starting point in Part IV: two paths (greenfield vs brownfield)
# converge into the same IVD cycle. Brownfield emphasized — most real teams have
# existing codebases. References IVD tools (ivd init, ivd_assess_coverage,
# ivd_list_features, ivd scaffold) that must match actual tool behavior.
#
# Reference: book/book_system_intent.yaml (system)
# Parent:    book_structure.part_4_adoption.chapters[0]
# Source:    framework.md (brownfield workflow, intent coverage assessment,
#            project_context), mcp_server/tools/scaffold.py, ivd_system_intent.yaml
# Version: 1.1
# Created: 2026-02-09
# Status: Pending
# ==============================================================================

parent_intent: "../../../book_system_intent.yaml"

scope:
  level: "module"
  type: "chapter"
  granularity: "coarse"

# ==============================================================================
# INTENT: What should this chapter accomplish?
# ==============================================================================

intent:
  summary: "Guide the reader through adopting IVD in their first engagement — two starting paths (greenfield vs brownfield) that converge into the same IVD cycle of intent → implement → verify, with emphasis on brownfield adoption using ivd init, ivd_assess_coverage, ivd_list_features, and ivd scaffold."

  goal: |
    By the end of this chapter, the reader:
    1. Understands the two adoption paths (greenfield vs brownfield) and why they converge
    2. Can bootstrap IVD in an existing project using ivd init (project context: code rules, architecture, tools, key_paths)
    3. Can assess intent coverage with ivd_assess_coverage and interpret the prioritized report
    4. Can check for existing features with ivd_list_features before building new ones (metadata, dedup)
    5. Can scaffold their first intent artifacts for high-priority uncovered modules (ivd scaffold with auto-linked parent_intent)
    6. Understands that coverage ≠ 100% — intent belongs where it adds value
    7. Has a concrete adoption checklist they can follow starting immediately
    
    This is the practical entry point for Part IV. The reader transitions from "I know IVD works" (Parts I–III) to "I'm using IVD on my actual project." Prose leads; examples illustrate.
    
    CRITICAL: "The First Week" is a conceptual scope describing the early adoption phase, not a time estimate. The chapter never implies a specific timeline.

  success_metric: |
    - Reader can initialize IVD in an existing project and explain what ivd init produces
    - Reader can interpret an ivd_assess_coverage report and prioritize which modules get intents first
    - Reader can check ivd_list_features to avoid duplicating existing features
    - Reader can scaffold module-level intents that auto-link to the system intent via parent_intent
    - Reader understands when NOT to add intent (

ose leads; examples illustrate.
    
    CRITICAL: "The First Week" is a conceptual scope describing the early adoption phase, not a time estimate. The chapter never implies a specific timeline.

  success_metric: |
    - Reader can initialize IVD in an existing project and explain what ivd init produces
    - Reader can interpret an ivd_assess_coverage report and prioritize which modules get intents first
    - Reader can check ivd_list_features to avoid duplicating existing features
    - Reader can scaffold module-level intents that auto-link to the system intent via parent_intent
    - Reader understands when NOT to add intent (simple utilities, stable code, config)
    - Reader has completed the adoption checklist at the end of the chapter
    - All tool references (ivd init, ivd_assess_coverage, ivd_list_features, ivd scaffold) match actual IVD tool behavior

  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_4 purpose: adopt and scale IVD in teams and organizations)"
    - "framework.md (brownfield workflow, intent coverage assessment, project_context)"
    - "mcp_server/mcp_server_intent.yaml (canonical tool definitions)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections (from book_system_intent Ch17)
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  sections:
    - id: "17.1"
      title: "Two Starting Points: Greenfield vs Brownfield"
      required_content:
        - "Greenfield: new projects start with a system intent and first module intent; conventions emerge as the project grows"
        - "Brownfield: existing projects with code, docs, and conventions already in place — the more common and more challenging path"
        - "Why both paths converge: regardless of starting point, the IVD cycle is the same (intent → implement → verify)"
        - "Brownfield emphasis: most real teams have existing codebases — the chapter focuses on this path because it's where adoption friction lives"
        - "What 'The First Week' means: early adoption scope — the activities you do when first bringing IVD into a project (NOT a timeline or deadline)"

    - id: "17.2"
      title: "Bootstrap IVD with Project Context (ivd init)"
      required_content:
        - "Run ivd init to scan the project and generate a system_intent.yaml with project context"
        - "What ivd init captures: code_rules (.cursorrules, lint configs), architecture (ARCHITECTURE.md, ADRs, principles), tools_scripts (key scripts, CLIs), libraries_reuse (internal shared libs), key_paths ('map to the stars': entrypoints, modules, workflows, docs, tests), existing_docs (README, API.md, CONTRIBUTING)"
        - "ivd init also detects agent instruction files (.cursorrules, .clinerules, .aider.conf.yml) and suggests adding IVD verification rules — the post-implementation verification protocol enforced at the agent level (see recipes/agent-rules-ivd.yaml)"
        - "Show example ivd init output: scanned artifacts, generated system_intent.yaml, project context sections, agent instruction file detection"
        - "Review and e

inciples), tools_scripts (key scripts, CLIs), libraries_reuse (internal shared libs), key_paths ('map to the stars': entrypoints, modules, workflows, docs, tests), existing_docs (README, API.md, CONTRIBUTING)"
        - "ivd init also detects agent instruction files (.cursorrules, .clinerules, .aider.conf.yml) and suggests adding IVD verification rules — the post-implementation verification protocol enforced at the agent level (see recipes/agent-rules-ivd.yaml)"
        - "Show example ivd init output: scanned artifacts, generated system_intent.yaml, project context sections, agent instruction file detection"
        - "Review and enrich: auto-generated context is a starting point — the team reviews and adds what the scanner missed"
        - "key_paths as 'map to the stars': the navigation index that tells any AI agent (or new team member) where to find what matters"
        - "system_intent.yaml becomes the parent_intent that all child intents reference"

    - id: "17.3"
      title: "Assess Intent Coverage: Know Where You Stand (ivd_assess_coverage)"
      required_content:
        - "Run ivd_assess_coverage to see which modules have intent artifacts and which don't"
        - "What the report shows: system-level check, module-level coverage, coverage percentage, priority suggestions (high/medium/low based on heuristics)"
        - "Priority heuristics: high = API, auth, agents, services, or 5+ code files; medium = 3–4 files; low = fewer"
        - "How to interpret the report: focus on high-priority uncovered modules — don't try to cover everything at once"
        - "Coverage ≠ 100%: intent belongs where it adds value — critical business logic, complex modules, team boundaries, frequently changed code"
        - "What doesn't need intent: simple utilities, configuration, stable code that rarely changes"

    - id: "17.4"
      title: "Check for Existing Features Before Building (ivd_list_features)"
      required_content:
        - "Use ivd_list_features to see what's already built — scans all *_intent.yaml files and returns aggregated feature list from metadata"
        - "Filter by category or tags to find relevant existing features"
        - "If a match exists: extend or reuse the existing feature, don't create a duplicate"
        - "Add metadata to new intents: feature_id (unique, snake_case), category, tags, status (implemented/planned/deprecated)"
        - "The feature inventory is always derived from intent metadata — no separate inventory file that can drift"
        - "Why this matters: in large or multi-team projects, knowing 'what already exists' prevents duplicate work"

    - id: "17.5"
      title: "First Intent Artifacts for High-Priority Modules (ivd scaffold)"
      required_content:
        - "Start with high-priority uncovered modules from the ivd_assess_coverage report"
        - "Run ivd scaffold --level module --name <module> to generate a module-level intent template"
        - "ivd scaffold auto-links parent_intent to the system_intent.yaml — child intents always reference their parent"
        - "AI loads project context (code rules, architecture, key_paths) when creating or modifying intents"
        - "Walk

r multi-team projects, knowing 'what already exists' prevents duplicate work"

    - id: "17.5"
      title: "First Intent Artifacts for High-Priority Modules (ivd scaffold)"
      required_content:
        - "Start with high-priority uncovered modules from the ivd_assess_coverage report"
        - "Run ivd scaffold --level module --name <module> to generate a module-level intent template"
        - "ivd scaffold auto-links parent_intent to the system_intent.yaml — child intents always reference their parent"
        - "AI loads project context (code rules, architecture, key_paths) when creating or modifying intents"
        - "Walk through scaffolding a first intent: from coverage report → scaffold → review → enrich with project-specific constraints"
        - "Start small: 1–3 high-priority modules, not the entire codebase"

    - id: "17.6"
      title: "Implement and Verify with Project Context"
      required_content:
        - "New intents follow project code rules captured in system_intent.yaml project_context"
        - "New intents reference architecture principles (ADRs, architecture docs) for consistency"
        - "New intents reuse existing tools, scripts, and libraries — no duplication of what's already in the project"
        - "key_paths navigation: AI agents use the 'map to the stars' to find related code, tests, docs"
        - "Verification: run constraints, check that implementation aligns with intent and project conventions"
        - "The full cycle in brownfield: ivd init → assess → check features → scaffold → implement → verify"

    - id: "17.7"
      title: "Retrospective: What Worked, What Didn't"
      required_content:
        - "Re-run ivd_assess_coverage: did coverage improve for the modules you targeted?"
        - "Check project_context accuracy: did the auto-generated context capture conventions correctly? What's missing?"
        - "Verify parent_intent links: are child intents referencing the system intent correctly?"
        - "Check reuse: are new implementations actually using existing tools and libraries?"
        - "Identify gaps in key_paths or architecture documentation that surfaced during adoption"
        - "What surprised you: common early-adoption discoveries (conventions you didn't know you had, duplicated features, missing docs)"

    - id: "17.8"
      title: "Setting Team Standards and Conventions"
      required_content:
        - "Update system_intent.yaml as conventions emerge from early adoption"
        - "Document discovered conventions in project_context sections (code_rules, architecture, tools_scripts)"
        - "The 'Intent First' habit: before writing code or asking AI to implement, write (or have AI write) the intent artifact"
        - "Agent rules: ensure all team members have IVD verification rules in their agent instruction files (.cursorrules, .clinerules) — the post-implementation verification protocol must be enforced at the agent level to close the IVD loop"
        - "When to create intents vs when to skip: critical paths, complex logic, team boundaries YES; trivial utilities, config NO"
        - "Onboarding new team members: the system intent + key_paths + intent arti

ctions (code_rules, architecture, tools_scripts)"
        - "The 'Intent First' habit: before writing code or asking AI to implement, write (or have AI write) the intent artifact"
        - "Agent rules: ensure all team members have IVD verification rules in their agent instruction files (.cursorrules, .clinerules) — the post-implementation verification protocol must be enforced at the agent level to close the IVD loop"
        - "When to create intents vs when to skip: critical paths, complex logic, team boundaries YES; trivial utilities, config NO"
        - "Onboarding new team members: the system intent + key_paths + intent artifacts are the onboarding documentation"
        - "Bridge to Chapter 18: from one team adopting IVD to scaling across the organization"

  manuscript:
    file: "chapter.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Key Points or Summary block at end (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Key Points reflect book_system_intent Ch17 key_points: two paths, ivd init, project context, ivd_assess_coverage, parent_intent, start small, coverage ≠ 100%"
      - "Adoption Checklist: step-by-step checklist the reader can follow immediately (constraint: checklist_included)"
      - "1–2 quotable lines where natural (constraint: quotable_principles)"
      - "Clear bridge to Chapter 18 (scaling across the organization)"
      - "All IVD tool references match actual tool behavior (constraint: tool_accuracy)"
      - "No timeline references — 'The First Week' is conceptual scope only (constraint: no_timeline_references)"

# ==============================================================================
# KEY POINTS (from book_system_intent Ch17; must be reflected in chapter)
# ==============================================================================

key_points:
  - "Two paths: greenfield (new projects) vs brownfield (existing projects) — both converge into the same IVD cycle"
  - "Brownfield: Initialize IVD with project context using ivd init — captures code rules, architecture, tools, key_paths, existing docs"
  - "ivd init detects agent instruction files (.cursorrules, .clinerules) and suggests adding IVD verification rules — the post-implementation verification protocol enforced at the agent level"
  - "Project context: the 'map to the stars' (key_paths) that tells AI agents and team members where to find what matters"
  - "Assess coverage: ivd_assess_coverage shows which modules have intent and which don't, with prioritized suggestions"
  - "Check before building: ivd_list_features shows what's already built from intent metadata — avoid duplicating features"
  - "Child intents reference parent_intent (system intent) — scaffolded automatically by ivd scaffold"
  - "Start small: high-priority uncovered modules first, expand gradually as you modify code"
  - "Coverage ≠ 100% — intent belongs where it adds value (critical logic, complex modules, team boundaries)"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement

dy built from intent metadata — avoid duplicating features"
  - "Child intents reference parent_intent (system intent) — scaffolded automatically by ivd scaffold"
  - "Start small: high-priority uncovered modules first, expand gradually as you modify code"
  - "Coverage ≠ 100% — intent belongs where it adds value (critical logic, complex modules, team boundaries)"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block and an adoption checklist"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section plus adoption checklist"
    test: "grep '## Key Points\\|## Summary' chapter.md returns at least 1 match; grep -i 'checklist\\|adoption checklist' chapter.md returns at least 1 match"

  - name: "quotable_principles"
    requirement: "1–2 quotable, memorable lines in chapter where natural"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.quotable_lines"
    validation: "Manual: at least one line is tweetable/memorable"
    test: "At least one bold standalone sentence in chapter.md works as a tweet/pull-quote"

  - name: "writing_style_part_4"
    requirement: "Prose leads; examples illustrate. Prose explains and generalizes. IVD tool examples support the narrative, not the other way around."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.code_and_prose.part_1_3_4"
    validation: "Manual: prose drives each section; tool commands and YAML examples appear as illustrations within the prose narrative"
    test: "Each section begins with prose explanation (not a code block); tool examples follow prose context; no section is code-only without narrative framing"

  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions: bold for key terms, italic for emphasis, code for paths/commands, paragraph structure."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions"
    validation: "Manual: formatting matches conventions"
    test: "Key terms bolded on first use; code font for file paths, commands, and tool names; no paragraph exceeds 10 sentences without subheading"

  - name: "tool_accuracy"
    requirement: "All IVD tool references (ivd init, ivd_assess_coverage, ivd_list_features, ivd scaffold) must match actual tool behavior as defined in framework.md and mcp_server/"
    source: "framework.md (brownfield workflow, intent coverage assessment); mcp_server/tools/scaffold.py; mcp_server/mcp_server_intent.yaml"
    validation: "Cross-reference every tool invocation, parameter, and output in the chapter against framework.md and mcp_server/ source"
    test: "diff chapter tool descriptions against framework.md §Brownfield, §Intent Coverage Assessment, §Feature Inventory; ivd scaffold flags and behavior match mcp_server/tools/scaffold.py; zero contradictions"

  - name: "checklist_included"
    requirement: "Chapter includes a concrete adoption c

tool behavior as defined in framework.md and mcp_server/"
    source: "framework.md (brownfield workflow, intent coverage assessment); mcp_server/tools/scaffold.py; mcp_server/mcp_server_intent.yaml"
    validation: "Cross-reference every tool invocation, parameter, and output in the chapter against framework.md and mcp_server/ source"
    test: "diff chapter tool descriptions against framework.md §Brownfield, §Intent Coverage Assessment, §Feature Inventory; ivd scaffold flags and behavior match mcp_server/tools/scaffold.py; zero contradictions"

  - name: "checklist_included"
    requirement: "Chapter includes a concrete adoption checklist the reader can follow immediately"
    source: "book_system_intent.clarity_tactics (checklists)"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a numbered or bulleted checklist with actionable adoption steps"
    test: "grep -i 'checklist' chapter.md returns at least 1 match; checklist contains at least 6 actionable items covering ivd init through retrospective"

  - name: "master_intent_alignment"
    requirement: "Chapter content must not contradict ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.master_intent_alignment"
    validation: "Manual review: Ch17 aligns with framework principles, tool definitions, and book structure; no contradictions"
    test: "No principle, tool behavior, or framework concept in chapter.md contradicts ivd_system_intent.yaml, book_system_intent.yaml, or framework.md"

  - name: "no_timeline_references"
    requirement: "Zero timeline references in chapter content (days, weeks, months as durations or deadlines). CRITICAL: 'The First Week' in the title is conceptual scope, not a time estimate — the chapter must never imply a specific timeline for adoption."
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_timeline_references; book_system_intent.part_4.ch17.note"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline|by day|by week|end of week' chapter.md (excluding title and note about conceptual scope) returns no matches"
    test: "rg -i '\\b(days|weeks|months|timeline|by day|by week|end of week|on day)\\b' chapter.md outside the title reference and conceptual-scope note returns 0 matches"

  - name: "no_unsourced_statistics"
    requirement: "No percentage claims, adoption statistics, or industry data without explicit source or caveat"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    validation: "Any numerical claim includes source citation or is labeled as illustrative/author experience"
    test: "grep -E '[0-9]+%' chapter.md — every match has adjacent source or 'illustrative'/'author experience' caveat"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 17 is the practical adoption entry point — two paths converge into the IVD cycle, with brownfield emphasized. Part IV sequence: Ch17 getting started (individual/team), Ch18 scaling (organization), Ch19 advanced patterns, Ch20 ecosystem."

  reasoning: |
    - Part IV purpose: adopt and scale IVD in teams and org

test: "grep -E '[0-9]+%' chapter.md — every match has adjacent source or 'illustrative'/'author experience' caveat"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 17 is the practical adoption entry point — two paths converge into the IVD cycle, with brownfield emphasized. Part IV sequence: Ch17 getting started (individual/team), Ch18 scaling (organization), Ch19 advanced patterns, Ch20 ecosystem."

  reasoning: |
    - Part IV purpose: adopt and scale IVD in teams and organizations. Ch17 is the entry point — the first thing a reader does when they decide to use IVD on their actual project.
    - Two paths (greenfield vs brownfield) because readers come from both situations, but brownfield is emphasized because most real teams have existing codebases with existing conventions, docs, and code.
    - Tool-centric approach: the chapter walks through actual IVD tools (ivd init, ivd_assess_coverage, ivd_list_features, ivd scaffold) because adoption is concrete, not theoretical. But prose leads — this is Part IV, not Part II.
    - "The First Week" is conceptual scope, not a timeline. The note in book_system_intent explicitly states this. The chapter must never create time pressure or imply adoption should happen in any specific timeframe.
    - Coverage ≠ 100% is a key message: teams often over-adopt (try to cover everything) or under-adopt (only cover what's easy). The chapter teaches where intent adds value.
    - Adoption checklist: the reader should leave with a concrete, actionable list they can follow on their next project.

  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_4, Ch17 key_points, placeholder); framework.md (brownfield workflow, intent coverage assessment, project_context); mcp_server/mcp_server_intent.yaml (tool definitions)"
  date: "2026-02-09"
  stakeholder: "IVD book author"

# ==============================================================================
# ALTERNATIVES
# ==============================================================================

alternatives:
  - name: "Start Part IV with organizational strategy (top-down adoption) instead of practical getting-started"
    rejected_because: "Adoption must be bottom-up first — a single developer or small team tries IVD on a real project before organizational strategy matters. Top-down without hands-on experience leads to policy without practice."
    when_it_works: "If the reader is a VP/Director who will mandate IVD without personally using it — but that's not our primary audience for Ch17."

  - name: "Combine greenfield and brownfield into separate chapters instead of one chapter with two paths"
    rejected_because: "The two paths converge quickly — after initialization, the IVD cycle is identical. Separate chapters would duplicate 70% of content (assess, scaffold, implement, verify, retrospective). One chapter with a clear fork at the start is cleaner."
    when_it_works: "If brownfield adoption were complex enough to warrant a full chapter on its own (e.g., migration from another framework)."

# ==========

VP/Director who will mandate IVD without personally using it — but that's not our primary audience for Ch17."

  - name: "Combine greenfield and brownfield into separate chapters instead of one chapter with two paths"
    rejected_because: "The two paths converge quickly — after initialization, the IVD cycle is identical. Separate chapters would duplicate 70% of content (assess, scaffold, implement, verify, retrospective). One chapter with a clear fork at the start is cleaner."
    when_it_works: "If brownfield adoption were complex enough to warrant a full chapter on its own (e.g., migration from another framework)."

# ==============================================================================
# RISKS
# ==============================================================================

risks:
  - condition: "IVD tool behavior changes after chapter is written (ivd init output, ivd_assess_coverage format, ivd scaffold flags)"
    action: "Pin chapter examples to specific tool behavior documented in framework.md; include note that tool output may vary with version; verify against mcp_server/ source before publication"
    monitor: "framework.md changelog; mcp_server/ commit history"
    severity: "high"

  - condition: "Reader interprets 'The First Week' as a literal timeline and feels pressure to complete adoption in a specific timeframe"
    action: "Explicitly state in section 17.1 that 'The First Week' is conceptual scope, not a time estimate; avoid all time-duration language; test: no_timeline_references constraint"
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'I felt rushed' or 'I couldn't finish in a week'"
    severity: "high"

  - condition: "Coverage ≠ 100% message is misunderstood as 'IVD is optional' or 'skip intent for most things'"
    action: "Frame clearly: intent adds value at critical paths, complex modules, team boundaries, and frequently changed code. Provide concrete examples of what needs intent vs what doesn't."
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'I wasn't sure when to add intent'"
    severity: "medium"

  - condition: "Tool examples feel too abstract without a real project to follow along with"
    action: "Use a realistic brownfield scenario throughout (e.g., an existing API service with multiple modules, some documented, some not); provide example ivd init output, coverage report, and scaffold result"
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'I couldn't picture this in my project'"
    severity: "medium"

  status: "pending"

    pending_steps:
      - "step_4_write_manuscript"
      - "

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION (cross-check with book intent, framework, and IVD tools)
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  cross_check:
    - check: "All key_points from book_system_intent Ch17 appear or are clearly implied in chapter"
      validates: "intent.success_metric"
    - check: "All deliverables.sections (17.1–17.8) are present with required_content"
      validates: "deliverables"
    - check: "Manuscript has Key Points/Summary block and adoption checklist"
      validates: "constraints.chapter_takeaways, constraints.checklist_included"
    - check: "ivd init description matches framework.md §Step 0 and mcp_server/tools/scaffold.py (project_context sections, output format)"
      validates: "constraints.tool_accuracy"
    - check: "ivd_assess_coverage description matches framework.md §Intent Coverage Assessment (report format, priority heuristics, coverage ≠ 100%)"
      validates: "constraints.tool_accuracy"
    - check: "ivd_list_features description matches framework.md §Feature Inventory and mcp_server/mcp_server_intent.yaml (metadata-derived, no separate file)"
      validates: "constraints.tool_accuracy"
    - check: "ivd scaffold description matches mcp_server/tools/scaffold.py (--level, --name flags, parent_intent auto-link)"
      validates: "constraints.tool_accuracy"
    - check: "Writing style: prose leads; tool examples illustrate (Part IV style, not Part II)"
      validates: "constraints.writing_style_part_4"
    - check: "Zero timeline references in chapter body — 'The First Week' is conceptual scope only"
      validates: "constraints.no_timeline_references"
    - check: "Bridge to Chapter 18 (scaling across the organization) present in 17.8"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[17.8]"

      validates: "deliverables.sections[17.8]"

changelog:
  - version: "1.1"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: "Added agent instruction file detection (ivd init) and IVD verification rules to sections 17.2 and 17.8. Added key_point about agent rules. ivd init now suggests adding post-implementation verification protocol to .cursorrules."
    reason: "Framework update: agent instruction files are IVD integration surfaces. Ch17 adoption workflow must include adding IVD rules to these files."
